title: Eriosyce esmeraldana
taxonomy: Eriosyce esmeraldana is classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Caryophyllales, the family Cactaceae, and the subfamily Cactoideae. It belongs to the genus Eriosyce. The genus Eriosyce is a group of cacti native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. The classification of Eriosyce esmeraldana is closely related to other species within the same genus. Understanding its classification helps in the conservation and study of this species.

intro: Eriosyce esmeraldana, also known as the Emerald Pygmy Cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. Eriosyce esmeraldana contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic properties.
